summary: Raise edge_threshold from 0.10 to 0.20 to filter out the underperforming mid_0.10-0.20 band and concentrate on higher-edge signals.
evidence: mid_0.10-0.20 band: n=15, hit_rate=6.7%, avg_per_trade_return=-5.44%, total_pnl_pct=-13.05% — well below overall and the minimum evidence threshold
high LLM confidence slice: n=8, hit_rate=0.0%, avg_ret=-100.00%, total_pnl_pct=-128.00% — 14.3pp below overall hit rate with n>=8
YES side: n=15, hit_rate=6.7%, avg_ret=-5.44% vs NO side n=6, hit_rate=33.3%, avg_ret=+100.52% — YES trades dominate the mid edge band driving losses
high_0.20-0.30 band: n=1 (insufficient alone), but very_high>=0.30 n=5 hit=20% still outperforms mid band's 6.7% despite losses, suggesting edge floor matters
Bankroll has declined from $100,000 to $38,458 (-61.5%), with mid-band trades (n=15) accounting for the majority of resolved trades and negative PnL
expected_impact: Eliminating the mid_0.10-0.20 band (which produced -13.05% total PnL across 15 trades) should reduce trade frequency and concentrate capital on higher-conviction signals with better historical hit rates.
rollback: Revert to 0.10 if fewer than 5 new trades are generated over the next 3 weeks, or if the hit rate on new trades at edge>=0.20 falls below 20% after n>=8 resolved trades.
